Lawless space has no rescue — carry medical/repair supplies or travel with a buddy.

data.name: Daymar Rally — How to Participate (Community Event)
data.requirements: A ground vehicle in your chosen division — Buggy (Cyclone/STV), Truck (Ursa/Spartan), or Bike (Dragonfly/Hoverquad/Nox/X1). Optional support ship to deliver the vehicle and a teammate to crew it (max two drivers + two support pilots per team). Fuel/repair planning for the long course.
data.rewards: In-game ship prize pack for each division's winners; mainly bragging rights and spectacle. No farmable aUEC.

steps.title: Register for the event
steps.detail: Sign up at daymarrally.com during the annual event window. The rally is organized by ATMO Esports with official rules, checkpoints and safety infrastructure.

steps.title: Pick a division and vehicle
steps.detail: Choose Buggy, Truck, or Bike and bring the matching vehicle. Assemble a team (up to two drivers + two support pilots).

steps.title: Get to the start on Daymar
steps.detail: Transport your vehicle to Daymar (load it into a cargo/vehicle ship and land at the start corridor).

steps.title: Run the course
steps.detail: Race the ~510 km corridor across deserts and canyons, passing three outposts plus Starfarer checkpoints and the Reclaimer checkpoint 'The Betty'. Hundreds of racers run simultaneously — expect chaos, crashes, and pirates.

steps.title: Reach the finish
steps.detail: Cross the finish line; division winners receive ship prize packs. Even non-winners get the full spectacle of Star Citizen's biggest community event.

tips: Bring a support pilot to ferry repairs/fuel and recover a flipped vehicle.
Drive conservatively through canyons — the course is as much about survival as speed.
Watch the calendar on daymarrally.com; it's an annual fixture, not always live.
Spectating is also worthwhile if you don't want to race.
Expect opportunistic PvP pirates along the route.

data.name: Post-Wipe Beginner Progression Roadmap (Alpha 4.8)
data.requirements: Fresh account or post-wipe save. Game package with a starter ship (Aurora Mk II recommended). Apply a referral code DURING account creation for +50,000 aUEC (stacks with the ~45k tutorial reward; do it within 24h, cannot be applied retroactively).
data.rewards: From 0 to a self-sustaining bankroll: first combat ship (F7C Hornet Mk II), a hauler (RAFT/Hull-B), then access to 1M+/hr loops and a 3M+/hr salvage endgame.

steps.title: 1. Maximize starting credits
steps.detail: Create the account with a referral code for the +50,000 aUEC enlistment bonus. With the tutorial reward you can land near ~95-135k aUEC on day one. Pick a home location with a good cargo hub - Lorville (Hurston) or Area18 (ArcCorp) are strong beginner cities.

steps.title: 2. Day-one cash with bunkers
steps.detail: Open Contracts (F1). Accept the global Call to Arms for passive pay-per-kill. Stack Foxwell Enforcement bunker-clear contracts near Orison. Clear NPCs, loot full medium-armor sets and weapons, resell in town. 3-4 runs over 20-30 min net 150-200k. Avoid friendly fire (Crime Stat).

steps.title: 3. Unlock the combat gauntlet
steps.detail: Go to mobiGlas > Contracts Manager > Mercenary tab > Combat Gauntlet. Run Scenarios 1, 2, 3 once each in your starter ship to unlock the chain. These are low-risk PvE with no reputation gate.

steps.title: 4. Buy your first real combat ship
steps.detail: With ~1-1.5M banked, buy and kit out an F7C Hornet Mk II - the best first serious combat buy. It clears Gilly's and Foxwell faster, repairs cheaper, and scales with weapon upgrades. Alternatives: Corsair, Constellation Taurus.

steps.title: 5. Farm the gauntlet for your bankroll
steps.detail: Rotate Scenarios 4, 5 and 7. Scenario 7 pays 200k+ and often spawns only light fighters - a clean run is ~500k in 10-15 min. Control angles, re-enter from side/rear to avoid fire. Sustained ~1M/hr is achievable. Collect Scrips for later 50:1 Wikelo Favor trades.

steps.title: 6. Pivot to hauling for consistency + reputation
steps.detail: Rent an Argo RAFT at Lorville Tissa Spaceport (67k/day or 181k/3 days) or buy one; grab a MaxLift tractor beam (~20k). Set spawn at a cargo hub (Seraphim Station / Everus Harbor). Complete one Covalex titanium delivery to unlock mission stacking.

steps.title: 7. Run stacked planetary haul loops
steps.detail: Accept 5-6 same-route Covalex missions. Load the cargo grid at the freight elevator (left-click grab, scroll to adjust height, neat stacks). Deliver in one trip (e.g. Seraphim -> August Dunlow). ~300k+ per loop; ~1.5M/hr at full speed. Builds Covalex rep toward Master (interstellar + military QD blueprints).

steps.title: 8. Add an owned salvage ship
steps.detail: Buy a Drake Vulture with Abrade modules. Scrape RMC from wrecks (target 8,000/6,000 signatures, dense panels first). RMC ~6,040/SCU; ~200-300k/hr solo, no rep gate. A good owned-ship loop while grinding Adagio reputation on the side.

steps.title: 9. Graduate to high-ceiling salvage contracts
steps.detail: Grind Adagio Holdings reputation from basic Hull C contracts up to Senior Contractor. Then run cargo-grab contracts on wrecked C2/M2/A2 Hercules with a RAFT - scan for intact grids, tractor valuable boxes, sell at TDD/Grim HEX. 3M+/hr realistic at Senior Contractor.

tips: Post-wipe rule #1: protect your bankroll. Buy ships that unlock income, not ones that just look good in the hangar.
Avoid Orison servers at peak hours - FPS tanks. Low server FPS is the biggest 4.8 frustration.
On a 30K crash mid-haul, do NOT immediately reclaim the ship - wait 10-15 min; the server often heartbeats cargo back to its last safe spot.
Use the Submit button for partial mission credit if cargo bugs out.
Set your spawn at a medical clinic (Transfer Imprint) near your work zone so Backspace respawns you on-site.
Watch rental timers; abandon any Grim HEX missions immediately as a fresh player.
Defense Con-style free rental events (e.g. Bevic Convention Center, Area18) periodically let you borrow Caterpillar/Vulture/Reclaimer/C2-class ships free - exploit them to multiply early income.

data.name: Stacked Covalex Planetary Hauling
data.requirements: Argo RAFT (rental ~67k/day or 181k/3 days at Lorville Tissa Spaceport, or owned) or Hull-B. MaxLift tractor beam (~20k). One completed Covalex titanium delivery to unlock stacking.
data.rewards: ~300k+ per 5-6 mission loop; up to ~1.5M aUEC/hr at full speed. Builds Covalex reputation toward Master (interstellar hauling + military quantum drive blueprints).

steps.title: 1. Set up spawn and ship
steps.detail: At a medical clinic (e.g. Seraphim Station) use Transfer Imprint so Backspace respawns you on-site. Rent/own a RAFT and fit a MaxLift tractor beam.

steps.title: 2. Unlock stacking
steps.detail: Complete your first Covalex Independent Contractors titanium delivery (Rookie rank) to enable holding multiple missions.

steps.title: 3. Stack same-route missions
steps.detail: Accept 5-6 Rookie-rank planetary titanium/cargo contracts that share a route. Everus Harbor / Hurston hub spawns dense overlapping Covalex contracts.

steps.title: 4. Load cargo
steps.detail: Position the RAFT cargo grid near the freight elevator. Tractor-grab boxes (left-click), scroll wheel to set height, stack neatly in the grid.

steps.title: 5. Deliver in one trip
steps.detail: Quantum to the destination (e.g. August Dunlow). Hold Left Alt + Middle Mouse to call ATC for a hangar, land, then use a quick-tap drop to place stable stacks under the freight elevator.

steps.title: 6. Submit and repeat
steps.detail: Complete all stacked contracts. If cargo bugs, hit Submit for partial credit. Repeat the loop; reputation climbs Trainee -> Rookie -> Junior -> Member -> Experienced -> Senior -> Master.

tips: Prioritize reputation over squeezing max profit early - higher Covalex ranks unlock far bigger contracts.
Stacking same-route missions and abusing short travel loops is the key to high hourly rates.
Member rank lets you hold many missions at once - a big efficiency jump.
On low server FPS, ghost/floating cargo can occur - wait for the server heartbeat to sync before reloading.

data.name: Piracy in 4.8 — Interdict, Disable & Steal Cargo
data.requirements: RSI Mantis (quantum dampener) to trap targets at SCM speed; a cargo ship (Cutlass Black / C2 / Caterpillar) to carry loot; ship or multi-tool tractor beam; FPS gear if boarding. A wingman strongly recommended.
data.rewards: Highly variable — a single loaded hauler can net several hundred thousand to 1M+ aUEC in stolen commodities; many runs net nothing. Generates CrimeStat.

steps.title: Pick a hunting ground
steps.detail: Camp known trade lanes and jump points where loaded haulers transit — Pyro lawless space and Stanton-Pyro jump approaches are prime. Lawless space avoids most security response.

steps.title: Scan and select a target
steps.detail: Use your ship scanner to identify cargo ships actually carrying freight. Prioritize haulers heavy with valuable commodities over empty or combat ships.

steps.title: Interdict with a quantum dampener
steps.detail: Drop the target out of quantum and pin them at SCM speed with the Mantis's quantum dampener so they cannot re-spool and flee. Note: 4.8 has reported bugs where interdiction sometimes fails to engage — be ready to abort.

steps.title: Negotiate or disable
steps.detail: 4.8's design rewards boarding/negotiation over destruction. Disable engines/thrusters precisely or demand they power down; outright destroying the ship often destroys the cargo you want.

steps.title: Board or tractor the cargo
steps.detail: If boarding, clear the crew with FPS weapons and tractor the cargo crates into your own hold; if disabling, pop the cargo grid and tractor externally. Work fast — distress calls draw responders.

steps.title: Haul out and fence the loot
steps.detail: Quantum away before reinforcements arrive and sell the stolen commodities at a no-questions terminal (Pyro outlaw stations / illicit trade terminals). Manage your CrimeStat before returning to lawful space.

tips: Interdiction can currently bug out and do nothing — keep a backup plan and don't over-commit fuel.
Two ships (one interdictor + one hauler) is far more effective than soloing both roles.
Empty ships waste your time — scan for cargo before committing to the interdiction.
Sell stolen goods at outlaw-friendly terminals to avoid impound/seizure.

data.name: Data Heist — microTech UGF Hack (Bit Zeros Rep)
data.requirements: FPS rifle + armor, medpens, a ship to reach microTech UGFs, and a Cryptokey (recommended for the terminal insert).
data.rewards: ~30,000 aUEC base variant up to ~60,000+ for higher Bit Zeros tiers; raises Bit Zeros reputation to unlock riskier, better-paid variants.

steps.title: Accept a Data Heist from a microTech contact
steps.detail: Data Heist contracts are only available around microTech. Take the lowest-difficulty variant first to build Bit Zeros rep.

steps.title: Travel to the designated UGF
steps.detail: Fly to the underground facility named in the contract and make your way to its datacenter.

steps.title: Insert the Cryptokey and start the hack
steps.detail: At the datacenter terminal, insert your Cryptokey to begin the mainframe upload for the hacker.

steps.title: Authorize the connection
steps.detail: Once the mainframe is hacked, allow the incoming connection from the mission giver to the mainframe to start the data transfer.

steps.title: Fix the overheating servers
steps.detail: Site owners trigger server overheats to stop the theft. Search the area, find why cooling failed, and re-enable the cooling systems to keep the upload alive.

steps.title: Survive the waves and extract
steps.detail: Enemies keep spawning throughout — hold the datacenter until the upload completes, then exfiltrate to your ship and turn the contract in.

tips: Bring a Cryptokey — it's the intended way to start the terminal hack.
Clear elevated angles before settling at the terminal; the upload pins you in place.
Stack medpens — sustained respawning waves are the real threat, not any single enemy.
Grind low-tier variants first; Bit Zeros rep gates the high-payout versions.

data.name: Box & Ryari Delivery Chain — Cutter Rambler Day-One Loop
data.requirements: Any small ship (MISC Cutter Rambler ideal), Greycat multi-tool with tractor-beam attachment, mobiglas to stack contracts. No combat gear needed.
data.rewards: ~20k aUEC per box mission; 100K-class delivery contracts chain in; full 'box → distro-center loot → Ryari → 100K delivery' chain can clear ~900K aUEC in one long no-combat session.

steps.title: Pull a stack of box-delivery contracts
steps.detail: At a major hub, accept several local delivery missions at once — they're lawful at novice rep and chain pickups/drop-offs around the same planet.

steps.title: Use a tractor multi-tool for boxes
steps.detail: Equip the Greycat multi-tool with the tractor-beam attachment so you can move multiple boxes without stopping to hand-grab each one — essential for multi-box missions.

steps.title: Run the deliveries in an optimized loop
steps.detail: Order drop-offs geographically so each landing serves several contracts. Any ship works; the Cutter Rambler's cargo + bed makes it the budget workhorse.

steps.title: Detour through a Distribution Center
steps.detail: When a route passes a DC, clear/loot it for free components and Citadel armor (see DC loot guide) to add value with zero extra travel.

steps.title: Collect Ryari harvestables
steps.detail: Pick up Ryari harvestables along the route to feed the higher-value 100K delivery contracts that unlock as your courier standing rises.

steps.title: Cash the 100K contracts
steps.detail: Turn in the stacked deliveries plus the 100K harvestable contracts to close the loop at ~900K for a long session — all without combat.

tips: Tractor multi-tool is the single biggest time-saver for multi-box missions.
Stack and geo-sort contracts before flying — don't take them one at a time.
Chaining a DC loot stop turns dead travel time into free gear.
This is the cleanest no-combat starter loop after the wipe.

data.name: Distribution Center Loot Farming (4.8) — Components & Citadel Armor
data.requirements: FPS rifle + armor + medpens, a ship to reach the DC, and backpack/cargo space to haul out looted components and armor.
data.rewards: Free ship components, Citadel armor sets, and FPS weapons (quietly added to DCs in 4.8); Expel Outlaws contracts also pay a cash reward.

steps.title: Take an Expel Outlaws / DC clear contract
steps.detail: Accept a Distribution Center combat contract from the security board, or simply travel to a DC known to spawn loot.

steps.title: Breach and clear elevated angles first
steps.detail: DC interiors are messy — NPCs perch on railings, platforms, and elevated walkways. Clear high angles before pushing into open rooms.

steps.title: Loot slowly and confirm each pickup
steps.detail: If you close the inventory screen too fast, looted armor can snap back onto the NPC body. Pull each item, wait, and confirm it stayed in your inventory before moving on.

steps.title: Prioritize components and Citadel armor
steps.detail: Grab ship components (high resale/crafting value) and Citadel armor pieces first, then weapons. These are the real reason to run DCs in 4.8.

steps.title: Haul the loot out
steps.detail: Carry components and armor back to your ship's hold; multiple trips may be needed for a full clear.

steps.title: Repeat across servers
steps.detail: DC loot is fixed per instance — re-clearing the same DC on different servers is the fastest way to farm components and armor.

tips: Confirm every loot pickup — fast inventory closes lose the item back to the body.
Components feed straight into the 4.8 crafting loop — don't vendor them blindly.
Citadel armor here saves you the Vaughn rep grind for the same gear.
Bring more medpens than you think; DC pushes are sustained FPS fights.

data.name: Medical Beacon Rescue Service — Cutlass Red / Apollo (50k per Save)
data.requirements: Medical ship — Drake Cutlass Red (budget: 2x Tier-3 beds + cargo) or RSI Apollo Triage/Medivac (gold standard: modular T1/T2/T3 rooms). Medical supplies/MedGels and a fast quantum drive.
data.rewards: Flat 50,000 aUEC paid from the rescued player's account per successful hospital delivery; builds medical/rescue reputation and player goodwill.
locations: Stanton - any hospital (Area18 / Lorville / New Babbage / Orison)

steps.title: Stage a medical ship and stock supplies
steps.detail: Spawn a Cutlass Red (affordable, two T3 beds + cargo) or an Apollo (modular rooms, handles severe/T1 cases). Load MedGels and medical supplies before going on call.

steps.title: Watch for medical beacons
steps.detail: Incapacitated players broadcast beacons. Accept one and quantum to their marked location — speed matters because they're bleeding out.

steps.title: Reach and revive the patient
steps.detail: Aim at the downed player and hold fire to run the revive beam through their bleed-out. When the bar fills they regain consciousness at very low health.

steps.title: Get them onto a medical bed
steps.detail: Move the patient into your medbay and use the Tier-3 (Cutlass Red) or higher-tier (Apollo) bed to stabilize and heal them up from low health.

steps.title: Transport to a hospital
steps.detail: Fly to the nearest hospital and deposit the patient. On delivery you're paid the flat 50,000 aUEC from their account.

steps.title: Return to standby
steps.detail: Restock supplies if used and watch for the next beacon. Apollo's extra beds let you handle multiple/severe patients per sortie.

tips: Cutlass Red is the cost-effective entry; Apollo is worth it only if you run high beacon volume or severe injuries.
Income tracks beacon availability — pair this with another loop for downtime.
Revive beam works through bleed-out — start it immediately, don't wait.
Keep MedGels stocked; arriving without supplies wastes the run.

data.name: Wildstar Racing — PU Time-Trial Rep & Reward Loop
data.requirements: A fast, agile ship (350r, M50, Razor / Razor LX, Fury LX, Mustang Gamma, P-52 Merlin) with a good quantum drive. No combat gear.
data.rewards: Cumulative aUEC per medal tier (bronze/silver/gold/platinum) — finishing a higher tier also pays the lower tiers plus the mission-fee refund; higher Wildstar rep unlocks new tracks.
locations: Stanton race tracks (community-built circuits added in 3.18, still active)

steps.title: Accept a Wildstar time trial
steps.detail: Pick up a time-trial contract from Wildstar Racing via the contract manager. Start with whatever tracks your current reputation has unlocked.

steps.title: Bring the right ship
steps.detail: Use a dedicated racer (M50, 350r, Razor LX, Fury LX). Agility and quantum/boost handling matter far more than firepower here.

steps.title: Learn the track line
steps.detail: Do a recon lap to learn gates and the racing line, then commit. Times are checkpoint-gated, so a clean line beats raw throttle.

steps.title: Beat the medal thresholds
steps.detail: Aim for the best medal you can hit — rewards are cumulative, so a gold finish also banks bronze/silver plus the returned mission fee.

steps.title: Climb Wildstar reputation
steps.detail: Repeat runs and better times raise Wildstar Racing rep, unlocking additional, higher-reward tracks over time.

tips: Rewards stack downward — always push for the highest medal you can land cleanly.
A recon lap to memorize gates is faster overall than blind speed runs.
This is a PU loop in your own ship — distinct from Arena Commander's instanced races.
Low risk and combat-free: a good change-of-pace earner between grinds.

data.name: Crafting & Fabrication Profit Loop (4.8) — Blueprints to Components
data.requirements: Access to a fabrication/crafting station, blueprints (from mission rewards/loot), and a supply of high-quality materials (mined ore in high quality bands, salvaged RMC, looted components). Strong inventory management.
data.rewards: Crafted ship components (power plants, coolers, shield generators, weapons) whose stats inherit material quality — sellable or tradeable to other players; blueprints themselves are high-value long-term assets.

steps.title: Acquire blueprints
steps.detail: Blueprints are the gate and the most valuable asset in the system. Farm them from mission rewards (e.g. Vaughn, Data Heist) and loot before chasing raw cash.

steps.title: Gather high-quality materials
steps.detail: Mining now uses 8 static quality bands — Band 8 (1000 quality) is premium crafting stock. Treat high-band ore, salvaged RMC, and looted components as feedstock, not vendor trash.

steps.title: Level your blueprint progression
steps.detail: Blueprint progression and material quality now matter more than before — invest in advancing blueprints since they outlast money through wipes.

steps.title: Fabricate components
steps.detail: At a crafting station, fabricate components (power plants, coolers, shield generators, weapons). Higher material quality carries through into better component stats.

steps.title: Sell or trade the output
steps.detail: Move finished components to other players or terminals. Quality-stat components command a premium over base-stat ones.

steps.title: Specialize and split roles
steps.detail: Solo: pair one gathering profession with crafting. Orgs: split gathering, refining, research, crafting, and logistics across members for throughput.

tips: Prioritize blueprints + reputation over short-term cash after the wipe — money resets, unlocks don't.
Band 8 / 1000-quality ore is premium feedstock; don't refine it into mediocre output.
Looted DC components feed straight into this loop — stop vendoring them.
For orgs, role specialization (gather/refine/research/craft/logistics) massively outpaces everyone doing everything.

data.name: Save Stanton / Fight for Pyro — Narrative Event Walkthrough
data.requirements: Combat-capable ship (fighter/gunship) and FPS gear for ground objectives. In Fight for Pyro, you must commit to one faction.
data.rewards: Faction-aligned event rewards (e.g. Pyro laser repeaters), aUEC from event missions, and one-time narrative progression.

steps.title: Join the Save Stanton defense
steps.detail: When active, Save Stanton tasks players with repelling a Slicer invasion attacking ships and stealing cargo across Stanton. Engage the invaders to earn event progress.

steps.title: Complete Stanton-phase objectives
steps.detail: Run the event missions broadcast during the invasion — defending convoys/locations and destroying Slicer forces to advance the narrative meter.

steps.title: Transition to Fight for Pyro
steps.detail: The continuation moves the conflict to Pyro. Here you must pick a side: Citizens for Prosperity, the Headhunters, or the Frontier Fighters.

steps.title: Choose a faction deliberately
steps.detail: Your allegiance is mutually exclusive and determines which missions you receive and which rewards you can earn — choose based on the reward you want (e.g. faction weapons).

steps.title: Complete your faction's mission line
steps.detail: Run your chosen faction's contracts toward its goal in Pyro to bank the event currency/rep and unlock the aligned rewards.

steps.title: Claim event rewards before it ends
steps.detail: These are limited-time/one-time-run events — collect the Pyro laser repeaters and other faction rewards before the event window closes.

tips: Faction choice in Fight for Pyro is exclusive — decide which reward you want first.
These are limited-time narrative events; don't expect them to be permanently available.
Bring both ship combat and FPS capability — objectives span space and ground.
Group up — invasion/faction objectives are tuned for multiplayer participation.
